reasoning: Polymarket cleared all regulatory hurdles in mid-2025 after CFTC and DOJ probes were dropped, leading to their $112 million acquisition of a licensed DCM, QCX LLC. On September 30, 2025, they filed self-certifications to list markets 'no earlier than October 2.' However, a US government shutdown began on October 1, pausing CFTC operations and temporarily halting the launch. Given the immense financial backing (a $2B investment from ICE in October valuing the company at $8B) and high prioritization from leadership, Polymarket US is positioned to launch almost immediately once the government reopens. The government shutdown would need to last an unprecedented 90+ days to push the launch past December 31, 2025. Given historical base rates for shutdown lengths, I assess a near-certainty (95%) that the government will reopen and Polymarket US will process its first trade before the end of 2025.
